label: Weather Warnings for Western Lake Superior

comments.text: MQT continues Marine Dense Fog Advisory for Lake Superior west of a line from Saxon Harbor WI to Grand Portage MN beyond 5NM [LS] till May 24, 11:00 AM EDT Link

Meshtastic update: I have my two nodes set up, and the one at home in Lakeview is seeing nodes as far as across Lake Michigan in Michigan proper WITHOUT MQTT bridging. For some reason, my office node is offline, but the home node is seeing all the downtown nodes. .bsky.social

MQT continues Freezing Spray Warning valid at Jan 18, 7:00 PM EST for Lake Superior west of a line from Saxon Harbor WI to Grand Portage MN beyond 5NM [LS] till Jan 19, 1:00 PM EST... at Sun, 18 Jan 2026 15:59:35 +0000 via IEMbot Additional Details Here.

The alternative explanation for the MI nodes I was seeing in Chicago - note that MQTT was turned off for ALL of this - is that a signal could have been bouncing off of a node on Indiana's south shore of Lake Michigan. I didn't see a large hop count, so whatever was happening was remarkable.
